title: verbascum × mixtum subsp. wirtgenii
intro: Verbascum × mixtum subsp. wirtgenii is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mullein family, known for its large, yellow flowers and thick, hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe coughs and respiratory issues, making it a popular ingredient in cough syrups and expectorants. The health benefits of Verbascum × mixtum subsp. wirtgenii include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function, as well as its potential to act as a natural antiseptic and antiviral agent. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, with the dried flowers and leaves often used in smoking blends to treat respiratory issues.
